content: Duress-PIN patterns have a well-documented failure mode: ATM SafetyPIN (reverse-PIN) was never adopted industry-wide because users forget or fumble alternate codes under real stress. Physical duress buttons mitigate false positives via double-press/simultaneous-press requirements and an 'I am safe' cancel toggle. Personal safety apps (bSafe) pair duress/panic triggers with a decoy 'fake incoming call' to let the user exit a situation without alerting an attacker.
